title: Mermaid Quay Multi-Storey Car Park - FD60 Steel Doorsets
excerpt: Design & Supply was selected to manufacture, supply and install fire-rated steel doorsets for the new Mermaid Quay multi-storey car park, a 679-space facility located at the heart of Cardiff Bay’s popular waterfront destination.
body:
OVERVIEW:

Design & Supply was selected to manufacture, supply and install fire-rated steel doorsets for the new Mermaid Quay multi-storey car park, a 679-space facility located at the heart of Cardiff Bay’s popular waterfront destination.The development has been designed to improve access for visitors while supporting the restaurants, shops and entertainment venues that make Mermaid Quay one of Cardiff’s most vibrant leisure destinations.As part of the wider construction package, Design & Supply delivered over 40 FD60 steel doorsets, tested and certified to EN 1634-1, providing 60 minutes of fire resistance in accordance with the project’s fire strategy requirements.

CHALLENGES:

Multi-storey car parks present a number of unique operational and compliance challenges:

  • Heavy Daily Usage – Doorsets must withstand constant use by large numbers of visitors.
  • Exposure to the Elements – External and semi-exposed locations require durable steel solutions capable of long-term performance.
  • Fire Compliance – The project required FD60-rated doorsets that met the building’s fire compartmentation strategy and relevant certification standards.
  • Long-Term Reliability – The client required a solution that would continue to perform effectively in a demanding, high-traffic environment.

OUR SOLUTION:

Design & Supply provided a complete package covering manufacture, supply and installation of the FD60 steel doorsets.

The doors were manufactured to achieve EN 1634-1 certification, delivering 60 minutes of fire resistance while providing the durability expected within a heavily used multi-storey car park environment.

By combining certified fire performance with durable steel construction, the solution was tailored to meet the practical demands of the facility without compromising on compliance or longevity.

RESULTS:

The completed installation provides a fully compliant fire-rated doorset solution for the Mermaid Quay multi-storey car park, supporting both the building’s fire strategy and its day-to-day operational requirements.

Key outcomes included:

  • Over 40 FD60 steel doorsets installed
  • EN 1634-1 certified fire performance
  • A durable solution for a high-traffic public environment
  • Long-term reliability without compromising compliance

The project demonstrates the importance of selecting doorsets that can deliver both certified fire performance and robust day-to-day durability in demanding multi-storey car park applications.

PROJECT SCOPE:

Supply and installation of fire-rated steel doorsets for the new Mermaid Quay multi-storey car park.

COMPLETION DATE:

2026

PRODUCTS USED:

Over 40 FD60 steel doorsets installed with EN 1634-1 certified fire performance

title: Watling Gardens Redevelopment - SR2 Security
excerpt: The Watling Gardens Estate redevelopment in Cricklewood, London NW2, delivered 125 new affordable homes across three newly constructed residential blocks, designed by Pollard Thomas Edwards Architects.
body:
OVERVIEW:

The Watling Gardens Estate redevelopment in Cricklewood, London NW2, delivered 125 new affordable homes across three newly constructed residential blocks, designed by Pollard Thomas Edwards Architects.The scheme comprises 80 general needs apartments and 45 one-bedroom independent living homes under Brent Council’s New Accommodation for Independent Living (NAIL) programme. The development provides modern, energy-efficient accommodation with integrated communal facilities and on-site care support for residents.

CHALLENGES:

As a high-profile affordable housing and independent living scheme, the project required doorsets that delivered:

  • Enhanced security performance suitable for residential blocks
  • Compliance with SR2 security standards
  • An architectural finish aligned with the design intent of the development

A key challenge was achieving SR2-rated protection without compromising the aesthetic vision of the scheme. The entrance doors needed to contribute positively to the building façade, rather than appearing purely functional or industrial.

OUR SOLUTION:

Design & Supply manufactured 21 bespoke LPS 1175 SR2-rated steel security doorsets with Lourve panels, along with 3 LPS 1175 SR2-rated glazed doorsets for designated areas within the blocks.

The standout feature of this package was the integration of a precision-engineered decorative panel design into the steel doorsets. The patterned perforated aesthetic requested by the client created a distinctive architectural statement while maintaining SR2 security performance.

This approach allowed the doors to:

  • Deliver certified SR2 resistance to protect against opportunistic break-in attempts
  • Enhance the visual identity of the entrances
  • Complement the contemporary brick façade of the development

By combining security engineering with architectural detailing, the doorsets achieved both performance and architectural design objectives.

RESULTS:

The completed installation demonstrates that high-security steel doors do not need to compromise on visual impact.

Our bespoke LPS 1175 SR2 doorsets contribute to a secure, modern residential environment while supporting the architectural quality of the Watling Gardens redevelopment. The project showcases how security-rated products can be engineered to meet demanding performance standards without compromising design intent.

PROJECT SCOPE:

Supply and installation of bespoke SR2 security-rated steel and glazed doorsets

COMPLETION DATE:

2025

PRODUCTS USED:

SBD LPS 1175 SR2-certified steel security doorsets and glazed steel doorsets in Jansen Janisol

title: Temple Quarter Enterprise Campus (TQEC) Research Hub
excerpt: The Temple Quarter Enterprise Campus (TQEC) Research Hub forms the first phase of the University of Bristol’s wider innovation campus, revitalising a post-industrial site near Bristol Temple Meads.
body:
OVERVIEW:

The Temple Quarter Enterprise Campus (TQEC) Research Hub forms the first phase of the University of Bristol’s wider innovation campus, revitalising a post-industrial site near Bristol Temple Meads. The project centres on the sensitive reinvention of two Victorian-era structures the Retort House (1821) and the Coal Shed (1885) located in a conservation area. Design & Supply was appointed to deliver a bespoke glazing package that balanced heritage preservation with high-performance modern requirements.Recognition: Shortlisted for the Schüco Excellence Awards

CHALLENGES:

  • Complex Heritage Integration: The biggest challenge was achieving precise installation of high-performance steel glazing into curved, irregular stone openings within listed buildings.
  • Technical Compliance: Meeting demanding thermal, acoustic, structural, and visual performance standards without compromising the heritage character.
  • Bespoke Components: Custom louvres, spandrel panels, and arched glazing units were developed to align with the aesthetic and structural needs of the buildings.
  • Specialist Installation: One key feature a 3m x 2m pane weighing over 500 kg required specialist lifting solutions and detailed installation planning.

OUR SOLUTION:

  • Design & Coordination: Our team collaborated closely with architects, engineers, and conservation officers to develop a fully coordinated, performance-driven façade package.
  • External Glazing: Supplied and installed arched-head screens fabricated from Schüco Janisol HI to match original stone openings, combining heritage accuracy with modern insulation and airtightness.
  • Custom Louvres & Panels: Designed and integrated curved louvres and bespoke insulated spandrel panels to maintain the building’s industrial language.
  • Internal Glazing: Delivered a suite of internal glass partitions and fire-rated doors using the Schüco Eco 60 and Janisol 2 systems, bringing light and function into the space while maintaining acoustic and accessibility standards.

RESULTS:

  • Award Shortlist: The project has been shortlisted for the Schüco Excellence Awards, recognising the technical complexity, heritage sensitivity, and design integration.
  • Energy Performance: Achieved low U-values and high thermal performance through intelligent specification and high-quality fabrication.
  • Architectural Fidelity: Preserved the visual integrity of the original structures while bringing them into the 21st century.
  • End-User Benefit: Delivered a research space that balances industrial character with comfort, usability, and durability.

PROJECT SCOPE:

External and internal glazing design, fabrication, and installation

PRODUCTS USED:

Schüco Janisol HI, Janisol 2, Eco 60, VISS Curtain Walling

title: Ebbw Vale Multi-Storey Car Park Security Upgrade
excerpt: The Ebbw Vale multi-storey car park project underscores Design and Supply’s expertise in delivering robust security solutions for high-traffic environments. In collaboration with Blaenau Gwent County Borough Council, we successfully installed 10 enhanced anti-vandalism doors tailored to meet the facility’s unique needs.
body:
OVERVIEW:

The Ebbw Vale multi-storey car park project underscores Design and Supply’s expertise in delivering robust security solutions for high-traffic environments. In collaboration with Blaenau Gwent County Borough Council, we successfully installed 10 enhanced anti-vandalism doors tailored to meet the facility’s unique needs.

CHALLENGES:

This project presented several key challenges:

  1. High Traffic Usage: The car park serves a large number of users daily, requiring solutions that can withstand heavy use.
  1. Vandalism Risks: The facility’s open-access nature made it prone to vandalism and unauthorised entry.
  1. Durability and Aesthetics: The doors needed to provide robust protection without compromising the car park’s visual appeal.

OUR SOLUTION:

  1. Tailored Anti-Vandalism Doors The enhanced anti-vandalism doors were customised to address the car park’s specific security and durability needs. Manufactured using high-strength materials, they provide effective protection against unauthorised access and vandalism.
  2. Expert Installation Our skilled team ensured a seamless installation process, delivering the project on time while minimising disruption to car park operations.
  3. Collaborative Approach By working closely with Blaenau Gwent County Borough Council, we ensured the final solution aligned perfectly with their security objectives and operational requirements.

RESULTS:

The successful installation of 10 anti-vandalism doors has transformed the Ebbw Vale multi-storey car park into a more secure and resilient facility. Key outcomes include:

  • Enhanced Security: The doors act as a robust barrier, significantly reducing unauthorised access and deterring vandalism.
  • Durability in Action: Constructed to endure the wear and tear of a high-traffic environment, the doors provide long-term reliability.
  • Positive Collaboration: The project demonstrates the value of close cooperation between Design and Supply and Blaenau Gwent County Borough Council.

JOB VALUE:

delivered and installed 10 enhanced fire-rated steel doors and heavy-duty anti-vandal security grilles

COMPLETION DATE:

November 2024

PRODUCTS USED:

Fire-Rated, Anti-Vandal Steel Doors & Security Grilles

title: Fish Island Village, London - Exterior Doors, Screens, and Curtain Walling Project
excerpt: Fish Island Village located in the heart of London, required an extensive upgrade to its external doors, screens, and curtain walling. The primary goal of this project was to not only enhance the aesthetics of the development but also to meet rigorous security and thermal performance standards.
body:
OVERVIEW:

Fish Island Village located in the heart of London, required an extensive upgrade to its external doors, screens, and curtain walling. The primary goal of this project was to not only enhance the aesthetics of the development but also to meet rigorous security and thermal performance standards.

CHALLENGES:

The project presented several challenges:

Stringent Security Standards: The project required compliance with Secured by Design (SBD) standards for external entrance doors.

Thermal Performance: The project demanded a range of U Values, from 1.0 W/m²K, to ensure compliance with thermal performance criteria.

Ventilation Solutions: The architects desired a specific aesthetic for external screens, and a solution for ventilation was required to maintain the desired look.

OUR SOLUTION:

We undertook a comprehensive review of the project’s specifications and developed a solution that not only met but exceeded the client’s expectations:

1. Secured by Design (SBD) Compliance

Recognising the need for SBD compliance for external entrance doors, we introduced our LPS2081 project. This product has been rigorously tested and certified within the Secured by Design initiative, ensuring the highest standards of security and peace of mind for residents.

2. Thermal Performance

Meeting the project’s diverse U Value requirements (ranging from 1.0 W/m²K) was crucial to ensuring the thermal performance of the external facades. Our experienced team selected and installed the Economy 60 and Janisol product lines, guaranteeing the desired thermal performance without compromising on aesthetics.

3. Ventilation Solutions

To satisfy the project’s unique ventilation requirements while maintaining the architect’s aesthetic vision, we incorporated carefully designed louvres into some of the external screens. This not only met the project’s practical needs but also contributed to the overall look of Fish Island Village.

RESULTS:

The project was successfully completed in August 2022, providing the following results:

Enhanced Security: Secured by Design (SBD) compliant entrance doors guarantee the safety and security of residents.

Optimal Thermal Performance: U Values ranging from 1.0 W/m²K ensure compliance with thermal performance standards, contributing to energy efficiency and comfort.

Aesthetic Satisfaction: The use of louvres in external screens satisfied the architect’s vision, balancing functionality with aesthetics.

PROJECT SCOPE:

Supply and Installation of All External Doors, Screens & Curtain Walling.

JOB VALUE:

£545,000.00

COMPLETION DATE:

August 2022

PRODUCTS USED:

Economy 60, Janisol, LPS2081, VISS.

title: The Delphi, Tenby - Steel Faced Doors SG1 and Security SR2
excerpt: The Delphi, a prestigious residential complex located in the scenic town of Tenby, sought to enhance its security while maintaining an aesthetic that echoed its coastal charm.
body:
OVERVIEW:

The Delphi, a prestigious residential complex located in the scenic town of Tenby, sought to enhance its security while maintaining an aesthetic that echoed its coastal charm.

CHALLENGES:

The challenge was to find doors that combined security with a design that seamlessly blended with the picturesque surroundings.

OUR SOLUTION:

Steel Faced Doors SG1 and SR2 Security The project team chose Steel Faced Doors SG1 for their aesthetic appeal and SR2 Security for their high-level security features. These doors provided the ideal balance of style and safety, aligning perfectly with The Delphi’s vision.

Key Features:

Coastal Aesthetic: SG1 doors were designed to match the coastal charm of Tenby, ensuring they blended seamlessly with the surroundings.

Security: SR2 Security doors offered a security rating, providing peace of mind for residents.

Customisable Options: Both sets of doors were customisable, allowing for adaptation to the unique architectural features of The Delphi.

Benefits:

Seamless Blending: The coastal aesthetic of SG1 doors harmonised with Tenby’s surroundings, enhancing the overall appeal of The Delphi.

Enhanced Security: SR2 Security doors provided a high level of protection, ensuring residents felt secure in their homes.

Tailored to the Setting: Customisability allowed the doors to be perfectly tailored to the architectural features of The Delphi, ensuring a seamless fit.

RESULTS:

The Delphi was no longer just a residential complex; it was a serene coastal retreat that offered security and style in perfect harmony. The Steel Faced Doors SG1 and SR2 Security doors elevated security while maintaining the picturesque appeal of the Tenby coastline.

PROJECT SCOPE:

Elevating Security and Style at The Delphi, Tenby with Steel Faced Doors SG1 and SR2 Security

PRODUCTS USED:

Steel Faced Doors SG1 and Security SR2

title: Silverstone Race Circuit
excerpt: The Silverstone Race Circuit, home to exhilarating motorsport events and the heartbeat of British racing, required a security upgrade to protect its valuable assets and ensure the safety of all personnel.
body:
OVERVIEW:

The Silverstone Race Circuit, home to exhilarating motorsport events and the heartbeat of British racing, required a security upgrade to protect its valuable assets and ensure the safety of all personnel.

CHALLENGES:

The challenge was to find a security solution that could withstand the rigorous demands of the racetrack.

OUR SOLUTION:

SR2 Security Rated Steel Face Doors The project team opted for SR2 Security Rated Steel Face Doors, renowned for their security features and resilience. These doors provided the ideal solution to safeguard the circuit’s assets and guarantee the security of this iconic motorsport venue.

Key Features:

High Security Rating: SR2 doors offered a security rating, ensuring the protection of valuable assets and facilities.

Durable Construction: These doors were designed with durability in mind, withstanding the heavy usage and high demands of the race circuit.

Customisable Options: The doors allowed for customisation to fit seamlessly with the circuit’s unique needs.

Benefits:

Enhanced Security: Silverstone Race Circuit’s security was significantly bolstered with the installation of SR2 Security Rated Steel Face Doors, deterring unauthorised access.

Durability: These doors proved their resilience in a high-traffic environment, standing up to the demands of the race circuit.

Tailored Solutions: The customisable options ensured that the doors seamlessly integrated with the circuit’s unique requirements.

RESULTS:

Silverstone Race Circuit became not only a world-renowned motorsport venue but also a prime example of how security and performance can harmoniously coexist. SR2 Security Rated Steel Face Doors elevated the circuit’s security standards while maintaining its reputation for excellence.

PROJECT SCOPE:

Enhancing Security at Silverstone with SR2 Security Rated Steel Face Doors

PRODUCTS USED:

SR2 Security Rated Steel Face Doors

title: Gower Road, Swansea - Transforming Home Spaces with Art’15
excerpt: The Gower Road, Swansea project is a testament to the transformation of private living spaces. A homeowner in Swansea approached us with a vision for revamping their home’s living area.
body:
OVERVIEW:

The Gower Road, Swansea project is a testament to the transformation of private living spaces. A homeowner in Swansea approached us with a vision for revamping their home’s living area. They sought a solution that would not only bring their idea to life but also enhance the overall aesthetic and functionality of their home. This case study highlights our collaboration with the client to turn their vision into reality using the versatile Art’15 system.

CHALLENGES:

The project introduced several challenges:

Aesthetic Transformation: The client sought to revamp their living area and create distinct partitions while maintaining a cohesive design.

Thermal Efficiency: The project required solutions that would provide thermal insulation to keep the living area cosy.

Heritage Style Design: The client desired a heritage-style design, and preserving the home’s heritage aesthetics was crucial.

OUR SOLUTION:

Our collaboration with the homeowner led to a creative and tailored solution:

1. Art’15 System Selection

Upon reviewing the client’s space and overall aesthetic, we suggested the Art’15 system. This system is ideal for internal residential spaces, with slim profiles and larger sight lines. The client requested samples, so we crafted corner samples with hinges and a working sample to demonstrate the operation. The client was captivated by the system’s features.

2. Bespoke Partitions

We provided two distinct partitions to create functional and aesthetically pleasing spaces. The first partition featured a fixed screen, separating the living area from the dining space. The second partition included a fixed screen with an integrated door, dividing the living area from the kitchen. The use of a 24mm double-glazed unit, providing a U Value of 1.6 W/m²K, ensured a cosy living area.

3. Heritage-Style Design

To preserve the heritage-style design, we incorporated self-adhesive glazing bars, which were affixed to the glazing during installation. This approach maintained the heritage aesthetics without compromising thermal performance. It offered a cost-effective alternative to welded profiles while achieving the desired design.

RESULTS:

The Gower Road, Swansea project was successfully completed in November 2020, resulting in several noteworthy achievements:

Aesthetic Transformation: The Art’15 system played a pivotal role in revamping the living area, creating distinct partitions while maintaining a cohesive design.

Thermal Comfort: With a U Value of 1.6 W/m²K, the partitions contributed to a cosy living space, ensuring thermal efficiency.

Heritage Preservation: The use of self-adhesive glazing bars preserved the heritage-style design without compromising thermal performance.

PROJECT SCOPE:

Creating Bespoke Living Spaces in a Private Residence

JOB VALUE:

£15,000.00

COMPLETION DATE:

November 2020

PRODUCTS USED:

Art’15
